steps.title: Identify the cost structure
steps.content: The first book costs 16 dollars. Each book after the first costs 11 dollars. If $x$ is the total number of books purchased, then $(x - 1)$ books are purchased at the 11-dollar rate.

steps.title: Build the equation
steps.content:
Total cost = cost of first book + cost of remaining books:
$$y = 16 + 11(x - 1) = 11(x - 1) + 16$$

steps.title: Verify with a test value
steps.content: For $x = 3$: $y = 11(2) + 16 = 22 + 16 = 38$. Check: first book 16 dollars + two more at 11 dollars each = 16 dollars + 22 dollars = 38 dollars. Correct.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = x + 16$: Charges 1 dollar per additional book instead of 11 dollars. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 11x + 16$: Charges 11 dollars for all $x$ books including the first, then adds 16 dollars on top. For $x = 1$: $y = 27$, but it should be 16 dollars. ✗
  • Choice C — $y = 11(x - 1) + 16$: 16 dollars for the first book and 11 dollars for each of the $(x - 1)$ additional books. For $x = 1$: $y = 16$. For $x = 2$: $y = 27$. ✓
  • Choice D — $y = (x - 1) + 16$: Charges 1 dollar per additional book instead of 11 dollars. ✗
C is correct.
